name: COTI
description: Manage COTI accounts, switch networks, and sign or verify messages. Deploy and manage private ERC20 tokens and ERC721 NFTs, including minting, transfers, approvals, and balance or supply checks. Call smart contracts and monitor transactions while keeping token values and metadata private.

tools.name: change_default_account
tools.description: Change the default account used for COTI blockchain operations. This allows switching between different accounts configured in the environment. The account must be configured in the environment variables with corresponding private and AES keys. Returns the new default account address upon successful

account_address.description: COTI account address to set as default, e.g., 0x0D7C5C1DA069fd7C1fAFBeb922482B2C7B15D273

tools.name: create_account
tools.description: Create a new COTI account with a randomly generated private key and AES key. Returns the new account address, private key, and AES key.

set_as_default.description: Optional, whether to set the new account as the default account. Default is false.

tools.name: decrypt_value
tools.description: Decrypt a value using the COTI AES key. Requires a ciphertext as input. Returns the decrypted value.

tools.name: get_current_network
tools.description: Get the currently configured COTI network (testnet or mainnet). Returns the network that all blockchain operations will use.

tools.name: encrypt_value
tools.description: Encrypt a value using the COTI AES key. This is used for encrypting values to be sent to another address. Requires a value, contract address, and function selector as input. Returns the signature.

function_selector.description: Function selector. To get the function selector, use the keccak256 hash of the function signature. For instance, for the transfer function of an ERC20 token, the function selector is '0xa9059cbb'.

tools.name: export_accounts
tools.description: Backup all available COTI accounts and export them as a JSON string for future import. Returns a JSON string that can be copied and used for importing later.

account_addresses.description: Optional list of account addresses to export. If not provided, all accounts will be exported.

include_sensitive_data.description: Optional, whether to include sensitive data (private keys and AES keys) in the output. Default is true.

tools.name: generate_aes_key
tools.description: Generate a new AES key for the current account. Returns the AES key.

account_address.description: The address of the account to generate the AES key for.

tools.name: import_accounts
tools.description: Import COTI accounts from a JSON backup string previously created with the export_accounts tool.

merge_with_existing.description: Whether to merge with existing accounts or replace them. Default is true (merge).

set_default_account.description: Optional address to set as the default account after import. If not provided, will use the default from the backup.

tools.name: list_accounts
tools.description: List all available COTI accounts configured in the environment. Returns the account addresses, current default account, and masked versions of the private and AES keys.

tools.name: sign_message
tools.description: Sign a message using the COTI private key. This creates a cryptographic signature that proves the message was signed by the owner of the private key. Requires a message to sign as input. Returns the signature.

tools.name: switch_network
tools.description: Switch between COTI testnet and mainnet networks. This changes the network for all subsequent blockchain operations. Valid networks are 'testnet' and 'mainnet'. Returns confirmation of the network switch.

network.description: Network to switch to - either 'testnet' or 'mainnet'

tools.name: verify_signature
tools.description: Verify a message signature and recover the address that signed it. This is used to determine who signed a specific message. Requires the original message and the signature as input. Returns the address that created the signature.

tools.name: approve_erc20_spender
tools.description: Approve another address to spend tokens on behalf of the owner. This is used for allowing another address (like a contract) to transfer your tokens. Requires token contract address, spender address, and amount as input. Returns the transaction hash upon successful approval.

gas_limit.description: Optional gas limit for the transaction

amount_wei.description: Amount of tokens to approve (in Wei)

token_address.description: ERC20 token contract address on COTI blockchain

spender_address.description: Address to approve as spender, e.g., 0x0D7C5C1DA069fd7C1fAFBeb922482B2C7B15D273

tools.name: deploy_private_erc20_contract
tools.description: Deploy a new standard private ERC20 token contract on the COTI blockchain. This creates a new private token with the specified name, symbol, and decimals. Returns the deployed contract address upon successful deployment.

symbol.description: Symbol of the token (typically 3-5 characters)

decimals.description: Number of decimals for the token

gas_limit.description: Optional gas limit for the deployment transaction

tools.name: get_private_erc20_allowance

token_address.description: ERC20 token contract address on COTI blockchain

spender_address.description: Address of the spender to check allowance for

token_address.description: ERC20 token contract address on COTI blockchain

token_address.description: ERC20 token contract address on COTI blockchain

Returns whether

sort.description: 정렬 (date: 접수일자, crp: 회사명, rpt: 보고서명) ※ 기본값 : date

corp_cls.description: 법인구분 (Y: 유가증권시장, K: 코스닥, N: 코넥스, E: 기타) ※ 없으면 전체조회, 복수조건 불가

sort_mth.description: 정렬방법 (asc: 오름차순, desc: 내림차순) ※ 기본값 : desc

pblntf_ty.description: 공시유형 // A : 정기공시, B : 주요사항보고, C : 발행공시, D : 지분공시, E : 기타공시, F : 외부감사관련, G : 펀드공시, H : 자산유동화, I : 거래소공시, J : 공정위공시

last_reprt_at.description: 최종보고서 검색여부 (Y 또는 N) 1) 기본값 : N(정정이 있는 경우 최종정정만 검색)

  codeList에 종목코드가 포함된 종목들의의 정보만 추출됩니다.
  basDd 하나당 KRX API를 한번씩 호출합니다.

  codeList에 종목코드가 포함된 종목들의의 정보만 추출되어 제공됩니다.
  basDd 하나당 KRX API를 한번씩 호출합니다.

name: Malaysia Transit MCP
description:
A Model Context Protocol server that taps access to real-time bus and train information across 12 Malaysian cities. Get live arrivals, vehicle tracking, and route details from Rapid KL, Rapid Penang, and various BAS.MY services across Malaysia.

Currently covering the following areas:

- Kangar
- Alor Setar
- Penang
- Klang Valley (Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Putrajaya)
- Melaka
- Johor Bahru
- Kota Bharu
- Kuala Terengganu
- Kuching
- Ipoh
- Seremban

More areas to be covered soon!

Note: this is NOT an official MCP server by the Government of Malaysia or anyone from Prasarana Malaysia, Ministry of Transport (MOT), Agensi Pengangkutan Awam Darat (APAD), KTM Berhad, or operators involved in BAS.MY operations.

tools.name: list_service_areas
tools.description: List all available transit service areas in Malaysia (e.g., Klang Valley, Penang, Kuantan)

tools.name: detect_location_area
tools.description: Automatically detect which transit service area a location belongs to using geocoding. Use this when the user mentions a place name without specifying the area (e.g., "KTM Alor Setar", "Komtar", "KLCC"). IMPORTANT: After detecting the area, use find_nearby_stops_with_arrivals or find_nearby_stops wi

location.description: Location name or place (e.g., "KTM Alor Setar", "Komtar", "Pavilion KL")

tools.name: get_area_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific transit service area

tools.name: search_stops
tools.description: Search for bus or train stops by name in a specific area. The middleware will automatically geocode place names (like "Ideal Foresta") and find nearby stops if no exact stop name match is found. IMPORTANT: If you are unsure which area a location belongs to, use detect_location_area first to automati

area.description: Service area ID (e.g., "penang", "klang-valley"). Use detect_location_area if unsure.

query.description: Search query - can be a stop name (e.g., "Komtar") OR a place name (e.g., "Ideal Foresta"). Place names will be auto-geocoded to find nearby stops.

tools.name: get_stop_details
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific bus or train stop

tools.name: get_stop_arrivals
tools.description: Get real-time arrival predictions for buses/trains at a specific stop

tools.name: find_nearby_stops
tools.description: Find bus or train stops near a specific location AND get all routes serving those stops. You can provide EITHER coordinates (lat/lon) OR a location name - the middleware will geocode place names automatically.

lat.description: Latitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

lon.description: Longitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

location.description: Place name to geocode (e.g., "Ideal Foresta", "KLCC", "Komtar"). Use this instead of lat/lon for convenience.

tools.name: find_nearby_stops_with_arrivals
tools.description: Find bus stops near a location AND get real-time arrival predictions in one call. RECOMMENDED: Use this tool when users ask about nearby bus stops and arrival times together. Accepts a place name (e.g., "Ideal Foresta") - the middleware handles geocoding automatically.

lat.description: Latitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

lon.description: Longitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

location.description: Place name to geocode (e.g., "Ideal Foresta", "KLCC", "Komtar")

tools.name: find_nearby_stops_with_routes
tools.description: Find bus stops near a location AND get all routes serving those stops in one call. Accepts a place name (e.g., "Ideal Foresta") - the middleware handles geocoding automatically.

lat.description: Latitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

lon.description: Longitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

location.description: Place name to geocode (e.g., "Ideal Foresta", "KLCC", "Komtar")

tools.name: list_routes
tools.description: List all available bus or train routes in a specific area

tools.name: get_route_stops
tools.description: Get all stops on a specific route. Use this to find which stops a bus/train route serves. IMPORTANT: First call list_routes to get the correct route_id.

tools.name: get_route_details
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific route including stops and geometry. IMPORTANT: First call list_routes to get the correct route_id.

tools.name: get_route_geometry
tools.description: Get the geographic path and stops for a specific route (for map visualization)

tools.name: get_live_vehicles
tools.description: Get real-time positions of all buses and trains in a specific area

tools.name: get_provider_status
tools.description: Check the operational status of transit providers in a specific area

tools.name: get_route_departures
tools.description: Get the next N departures for a specific route (both directions). Useful for showing upcoming bus/train times. IMPORTANT: Use route_short_name (e.g., "K10", "A32", "R10", "101") NOT the numeric route_id. Works for: penang, ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, joh

routeId.description: Route SHORT NAME (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") - NOT the numeric route_id. Get this from list_routes route_short_name field.

tools.name: get_next_departure
tools.description: Get the single next departure for a route in a specific direction. Quick way to find when the next bus/train leaves. IMPORTANT: Use route_short_name (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") NOT numeric route_id. Works for: penang, ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, johor, k

routeId.description: Route SHORT NAME (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") - NOT numeric route_id

tools.name: get_stop_routes
tools.description: Get all routes serving a specific stop with their next departures. Shows which buses/trains stop here and when. Works for: penang, ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, johor, kuching.

tools.name: get_route_schedule
tools.description: Get the complete daily schedule for a route. Shows all departure times throughout the day. IMPORTANT: Use route_short_name (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") NOT numeric route_id. Works for: penang, ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, johor, kuching.

routeId.description: Route SHORT NAME (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") - NOT numeric route_id

tools.name: get_route_origin
tools.description: Get the origin stop name for a route in a specific direction. Useful for showing where the bus/train starts. IMPORTANT: Use route_short_name (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") NOT numeric route_id. Works for: penang, ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, johor, kuching.

routeId.description: Route SHORT NAME (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") - NOT numeric route_id

tools.name: get_route_status
tools.description: Check if a route is currently operating based on its schedule. Shows if buses/trains are running now. IMPORTANT: Use route_short_name (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") NOT numeric route_id. Works for: penang, ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, johor, kuching.

routeId.description: Route SHORT NAME (e.g., "101", "K10", "A32") - NOT numeric route_id

tools.name: get_fare_routes
tools.description: Get all routes available for fare calculation in a specific area. MUST call this FIRST before calculate_fare to get valid route_id values. Supports: ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, johor, kuching, penang.

area.description: Service area ID - must be: ipoh, seremban, kangar, alor-setar, kota-bharu, kuala-terengganu, melaka, johor, kuching, or penang

tools.name: get_route_stops_for_fare
tools.description: Get all stops on a route with their distances for fare calculation. MUST call this SECOND (after get_fare_routes) to get valid stop_id values for calculate_fare. Returns stop IDs and names.

area.description: Service area ID - same as used in get_fare_routes

routeId.description: The route_id value from get_fare_routes response (e.g., "D62", "A32", "M15CWLMYMK")

tools.name: calculate_fare
tools.description: Calculate the bus fare between two stops on a route. IMPORTANT: You MUST first call get_fare_routes to get route_id, then get_route_stops_for_fare to get valid stop_id values. Do NOT guess IDs.

area.description: Service area ID - same as used in previous calls

toStop.description: The exact stop_id from get_route_stops_for_fare response

routeId.description: The exact route_id from get_fare_routes (NOT route_short_name)

fromStop.description: The exact stop_id from get_route_stops_for_fare response

tools.name: calculate_journey_fare
tools.description: Calculate the total fare for a multi-leg journey with bus transfers. Each leg is a separate fare since BAS.MY does not have integrated transfers.

area.description: Base service area ID (legs can specify different areas for inter-area journeys)

areaId.description: Area ID for this leg (optional, defaults to base area)

tools.name: get_route_directions_for_fare
tools.description: Get available directions for a route when calculating fares. Use this to determine which direction (outbound/inbound) to use for fare calculation.

tools.name: get_ktm_komuter_stations
tools.description: Get all 23 KTM Komuter Utara stations (Padang Besar - Butterworth - Ipoh line). Returns station codes, names, and coordinates.

tools.name: calculate_ktm_komuter_fare
tools.description: Calculate KTM Komuter Utara fare between two stations. Use station codes (e.g., "BU" for Butterworth, "IP" for Ipoh, "PB" for Padang Besar).

tools.name: get_ktm_komuter_fare_matrix
tools.description: Get the full KTM Komuter Utara fare matrix showing fares between all station pairs.

tools.name: get_ktm_station_departures
tools.description: Get departure times for a specific KTM station. Supports both KTM Komuter Utara and KTM Intercity schedules.

type.description: Schedule type: ktm-komuter-utara (Padang Besar-Ipoh) or ktm-intercity (SH/ERT routes)

tools.name: get_ktm_stations
tools.description: Get all KTM stations for a specific schedule type (Komuter Utara or Intercity).

tools.name: get_ktm_schedules
tools.description: Get full KTM schedule data for a specific schedule type. Returns complete timetable information.

tools.name: find_nearby_ktm_stations
tools.description: Find KTM stations near a specific location. You can provide EITHER coordinates (lat/lon) OR a location name - the middleware will geocode place names automatically.

lat.description: Latitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

lon.description: Longitude coordinate (optional if location is provided)

location.description: Place name to geocode (e.g., "Butterworth", "Ipoh", "Padang Besar"). Use this instead of lat/lon for convenience.

tools.name: get_penang_ferry_overview
tools.description: Get Penang Ferry service overview including terminals, operating hours, frequency, and contact information. The ferry operates between Butterworth and George Town.

tools.name: get_penang_ferry_schedule
tools.description: Get full Penang Ferry schedule with departure times. Supports filtering by direction and day type.

tools.name: get_penang_ferry_next_departure
tools.description: Get next ferry departures from both Butterworth and George Town terminals in real-time. Shows minutes until departure.

tools.name: get_penang_ferry_terminals
tools.description: Get detailed information about Penang Ferry terminals including facilities, connections, parking, and nearby attractions.

tools.name: get_penang_ferry_fare
tools.description: Get Penang Ferry fare information, payment methods, and terminal coordinates.

tools.name: get_system_health
tools.description: Check the health status of the Malaysia Transit middleware service

tools.name: get_debug_info
tools.description: Get comprehensive debug information about the middleware service including memory usage and initialized areas

tools.name: get_api_analytics
tools.description: Get API usage analytics and statistics from the middleware. Shows total requests, requests per hour, error rates, and usage by service area. Useful for monitoring API health and usage patterns.

type.description: Type of analytics: summary (overview), endpoints (per-endpoint stats), areas (per-area stats), cumulative (all-time totals), clients (app/website usage)

tools.name: get_area_analytics
tools.description: Get detailed API usage analytics for a specific service area. Shows which endpoints are most used for that area.

tools.name: hello
tools.description: A simple test tool to verify that the MCP server is working correctly

name: V-Sekai-fire's Minizinc
description: Solve constraint programming problems with MiniZinc.

tools.name: minizinc_solve
tools.description:
Solves a MiniZinc model using chuffed solver (fixed, not configurable).

Standard libraries: By default, automatically includes common MiniZinc standard libraries (e.g., alldifferent.mzn) 
if not already present in the model. This can be controlled via the auto_include_stdlib parameter (default: tru

timeout.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000, i.e., 30 seconds). Maximum allowed is 30000 ms (30 seconds); values exceeding this will be capped at 30 seconds.

data_content.description: Optional .dzn data content as string. Must be valid DZN format (e.g., 'n = 8;'). Parsed and included in response.

auto_include_stdlib.description: Automatically include standard MiniZinc libraries (e.g., alldifferent.mzn) if not present (default: true)

tools.name: minizinc_validate
tools.description:
Validates a MiniZinc model by checking syntax and type checking without solving.
Useful for debugging models before attempting to solve them.

Returns detailed error and warning messages if the model is invalid.

data_content.description: Optional .dzn data content as string. Must be valid DZN format (e.g., 'n = 8;').

auto_include_stdlib.description: Automatically include standard MiniZinc libraries (e.g., alldifferent.mzn) if not present (default: true)

name: DinCoder
description:
Driven Intent Negotiation — Contract-Oriented Deterministic Executable Runtime

IMPORTANT: 
> - **Using Claude Code?** → Install the [Plugin](#-claude-code-plugin-recommended-for-claude-code) (easier, includes slash commands & agents)
> - **Using VS Code/Codex/Cursor?** → Install [MCP Server Only](#installing-via-smithery) (plugins not supported)
>
> ⚠️ **Don't install both!** The plugin automatically installs the MCP server - installing both may cause conflicts.

DinCoder brings the power of GitHub Spec Kit to any AI coding agent through the Model Context Protocol. It transforms the traditional "prompt-then-code-dump" workflow into a systematic, specification-driven process where specifications don't serve code—code serves specifications.

🌟 The Power Inversion: A New Development Paradigm
For decades, code has been king. Specifications were scaffolding—built, used, then discarded once "real work" began. PRDs guided development, design docs informed implementation, but these were always subordinate to code. Code was truth. Everything else was, at best, good intentions.

Spec-Driven Development inverts this power structure: 

Specifications Generate Code: The PRD isn't a guide—it's the source that produces implementation
Executable Specifications: Precise, complete specs that eliminate the gap between intent and implementation
Code as Expression: Code becomes the specification's expression in a particular language/framework
Living Documentation: Maintain software by evolving specifications, not manually updating code
This transformation is possible because AI can understand complex specifications and implement them systematically. But raw AI generation without structure produces chaos. DinCoder provides that structure through GitHub's proven Spec Kit methodology. 

Plugin install :
**Step 1: Add the DinCoder marketplace**
```bash
# In Claude Code
/plugin marketplace add flight505/dincoder-plugin
```

**Step 2: Install the plugin**
```bash
/plugin install dincoder
```

id.description: Field ID/alias

type.description:
SingleSelect/MultipleSelect: 1=do not add new options, 2=allow adding new options, default is 1.
Attachment: 0=overwrite, 1=add new, default is 0.

value.description:
Depending on the type, the value field supports the following parameter formats:

1. **type=Attachment（附件 / Attachment）**:
   Pass an array of objects, where each object contains `name` and `url`.
   Example / 示例：[{"name":"file_name_with_extension / 文件名称，带后缀","url":"url/base64"}]

2. **type=Relation/Collaborator/Department/OrgRole（关系 / 合作者 / 部门 / 组织角色）**:
   Pass an array of text strings.
   Example / 示例：["UserA / 用户A", "UserB / 用户B"]

3. **type=SingleSelect/MultipleSelect（单选 / 多选）**:
   Pass an array of option `value`s.
   Example / 示例：["Option1 / 选项值1", "Option2 / 选项值2"]

4. **type=Region（地区 / Region）**:
   Pass a region code string (obtainable via the region information API / 可通过获取地区信息接口查询)。
   Example / 示例："310100"

5. **type=Location（定位 / Location）**:
   Pass a JSON string containing four fields: `x`, `y`, `address`, and `title`.
   Example / 示例：`{"x":66.666666,"y":88.888888,"address":"Caohejing Development Zone, Shanghai / 上海市漕河泾开发区","title":"Company Building / 公司大楼"}`

6. **type=Checkbox（复选框 / Checkbox）**:
   Pass 1 to indicate checked / 传入 1 表示选中，0 to indicate unchecked / 传入 0 表示未选中。
   Example / 示例：1

7. **type=Rating（等级 / Rating）**:
   Pass a numeric string within the valid range / 传入范围内的数字字符串。
   Example / 示例："5"

8. **Other types（其他类型 / Other types）**:
   Typically pass a string or numeric value / 通常传入字符串或数值。

ai_description.description: e.g. Worksheet: <Worksheet Name>. Response in user's language.

field.title: Field ID or alias, field ID is preferred, if there is no alias, field ID is used directly

isAsc.title: Whether to sort in ascending order, default is descending

fields.description: Specify an array of field IDs to return. Only these fields will be included in the response. Optional.

pageSize.description: Number of items per page, max 1000

tableView.description: Whether to return record data in table view format

ai_description.description: Description of the query parameters, including worksheet, view (if any), filter conditions, sorting, etc. Output detailed field names and condition operators/values. Response in user's language.

useFieldIdAsKey.description: Whether to use field ID as the key for returned data, default is to use alias

includeTotalCount.description: Whether to return the total number of records

tools.name: get_record_pivot_data
tools.description: Get pivot table summary data for worksheet records. Prioritize using this tool for querying statistics, summaries, and aggregations.

granularity.description:
Date and region statistical dimension.
When the field type is date or region, use this parameter to set the statistical grouping granularity.
- Date/Time: 1-Day, 2-Week, 3-Month;
- Region: 1-Province, 2-Province/City, 3-Province/City/County (District).

includeEmpty.description: Whether to include empty values in statistics, default is false.

isAsc.description: Whether to sort in ascending order, default is descending

aggregation.description:
Aggregation method (case-insensitive)
- COUNT: Count
- DISTINCTCOUNT: Distinct Count
- SUM: Sum
- MIN: Minimum
- MAX: Maximum
- AVG: Average

includeEmpty.description: Whether to include empty values in statistics, default is false. Empty values will be displayed as 0.

granularity.description:
Date and region statistical dimension.
When the field type is date or region, use this parameter to set the statistical grouping granularity.
- Date/Time: 1-Day, 2-Week, 3-Month;
- Region: 1-Province, 2-Province/City, 3-Province/City/County (District).

includeEmpty.description: Whether to include empty values in statistics, default is false.

pageSize.description: Number of items per page, max 1000

ai_description.description: Description of the query parameters, including worksheet, view (if any), filter conditions, dimensions, values, sorting, etc. Output detailed field names and condition operators/values. Response in user's language.

includeSummary.description: Whether to include a summary of all rows in the return value

name: Terminal MCP Server
description: Execute commands on local or remote hosts seamlessly. Leverage a powerful interface to run system commands, manage sessions, and set environment variables effortlessly. Enhance your AI models and applications with robust command execution capabilities.

tools.name: execute_command
tools.description: Execute commands on remote hosts or locally (This tool can be used for both remote hosts and the current machine)

host.description: Host to connect to (optional, if not provided the command will be executed locally)

command.description: Command to execute. Before running commands, it's best to determine the system type (Mac, Linux, etc.)

session.description: Session name, defaults to 'default'. The same session name will reuse the same terminal environment for 20 minutes, which is useful for operations requiring specific environments like conda.

username.description: Username for SSH connection (required when host is specified)

name: Hyperion Blockchain Server
description: Enable seamless interaction with the Hyperion blockchain through a standardized MCP interface. Manage wallets, send transactions, query blockchain data, and interact with smart contracts effortlessly. Simplify building and integrating Hyperion-based applications with comprehensive developer tools and robust error handling.

tools.name: create_wallet
tools.description: Create a new Hyperion wallet with a generated mnemonic phrase

tools.name: import_wallet
tools.description: Import an existing wallet using private key or mnemonic phrase

mnemonic.description: Mnemonic phrase to import (alternative to private key)

privateKey.description: Private key to import (alternative to mnemonic)

tools.name: list_wallets
tools.description: List all available wallets

tools.name: get_balance
tools.description: Get the balance of a wallet address (native tokens or ERC20 tokens)

address.description: Wallet address to check balance for

tools.name: get_native_balance
tools.description: Get the native tMETIS balance of a wallet address on Hyperion testnet

address.description: Wallet address to check native balance for

tools.name: send_transaction
tools.description: Send native tokens or ERC20 tokens to another address

amount.description: Amount to send (in token units, not wei)

tokenAddress.description: Optional ERC20 token contract address (for token transfers)

tools.name: get_network_info
tools.description: Get current network information and status

tools.name: get_transaction
tools.description: Get details of a transaction by hash

tools.name: get_block
tools.description: Get block information by number or hash

tools.name: estimate_gas
tools.description: Estimate gas cost for a transaction

data.description: Optional transaction data

value.description: Optional value to send (in ether)

tools.name: set_current_wallet
tools.description: Set the current active wallet for transactions

address.description: Wallet address to set as current

tools.name: get_current_wallet
tools.description: Get the current active wallet information

tools.name: deploy_erc20_token
tools.description: Deploy a new ERC20 token contract

symbol.description: Token symbol (e.g., 'MTK')

mintable.description: Whether token should be mintable (default: true)

tools.name: deploy_erc721_token
tools.description: Deploy a new ERC721 token contract (NFT)

symbol.description: Token symbol (e.g., 'NFT')

mintable.description: Whether the token should be mintable (default: false)

tools.name: get_token_info
tools.description: Get comprehensive ERC20 token information including name, symbol, decimals, supply, and owner

tools.name: mint_tokens
tools.description: Mint additional tokens for mintable ERC20 contracts (owner only)

tools.name: get_nft_info
tools.description: Get comprehensive ERC721 NFT information including collection details and specific token info

tokenId.description: Optional token ID to get specific token info

tools.name: mint_nft
tools.description: Mint a new NFT for mintable ERC721 contracts (owner only)

tokenId.description: Unique token ID for the new NFT

tools.name: get_dat_contract
tools.description: Get the pre-deployed DAT (Data Anchoring Token) contract address on the LazAI testnet. This contract is managed by the Alith SDK.

tools.name: register_dat_file
tools.description: Register a file URL with LazAI to get a fileId. This is the first step in the DAT minting workflow. After registering, you can request proof and then claim the DAT reward.

url.description: The URL of the file to register (IPFS or HTTP URL)

tools.name: request_dat_proof
tools.description: Request proof verification for a registered file from LazAI verified computing nodes. This is required before claiming a DAT reward.

value.description: Optional value to send with the request (in wei)

fileId.description: The file ID returned from register_dat_file

tools.name: claim_dat_reward
tools.description: Claim a DAT (Data Anchoring Token) reward for a registered file. This mints the DAT token after the file has been verified by computing nodes (proofs > 0).

name: Lano-enterprise-mcp
description: Store and retrieve semantic memories using advanced vector search and intelligence-driven insight extraction. Organize knowledge across projects with secure access controls and comprehensive system health monitoring. Optimize your information flow by detecting duplicates and analyzing usage patterns for better decision support.

name: AI Answer Copier
description:
AI Answer Copier is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that solves the "Final Mile" friction in educational content creation. It enables AI models to move beyond just writing questions to actually generating the files required for teaching and assessment.

By functioning as a native MCP server, this tool allows Claude or any MCP-enabled IDE to directly pipe its output into specialized educational formats. No more manually fixing bullet points in Word or wrestling with CSV headers for your LMS.

Key Capabilities:Zero-Friction Pipeline: Your AI can now "see" your local export tools. Ask it to: "Generate 10 biology questions and send them directly to my Quizizz CSV."

Multi-Format Exporting: Seamlessly convert AI responses into upload-ready files for Kahoot, Quizizz, Canvas (JSON), Moodle (XML), and professionally formatted PDFs.

Intelligent Smart-Parse: Automatically identifies question stems, multiple-choice distractors, and correct answer keys from raw AI text.

First-Class Math & Code: Native support for LaTeX equations ($\sqrt{x}$) and indented code snippets (Python/C++), ensuring they don't break during the export

Why use this MCP server? Generating questions takes seconds, but formatting them takes hours. This server reclaims those 5 hours of your week by removing the technical barrier between AI intelligence and classroom delivery.

name: svelte-llm-mcp
description: Connect your AI coding assistant directly to up-to-date Svelte 5 and SvelteKit documentation via this MCP server.

tools.name: list_sections
tools.description: Lists all available Svelte 5 and SvelteKit documentation sections in a structured format. Returns sections as a list of "* title: [section_title], path: [file_path]" - you can use either the title or path when querying a specific section via the get_documentation tool. Always run list_sections first

tools.name: get_documentation
tools.description: Retrieves full documentation content for Svelte 5 or SvelteKit sections. Supports flexible search by title (e.g., "$state", "routing") or file path (e.g., "docs/svelte/state.md"). Can accept a single section name or an array of sections. Before running this, make sure to analyze the users query, as

section.description: The section name(s) to retrieve. Can search by title (e.g., "$state", "load functions") or file path (e.g., "docs/svelte/state.md"). Supports single string and array of strings

name: plsreadme
description: Transform markdown content into clean, readable web links for easy sharing. Generate permanent public URLs for READMEs, documentation, and project proposals. Streamline collaboration by providing a professional web view for your markdown files.

tools.name: plsreadme_share_text
tools.description:
Share markdown text as a clean, readable web link on plsreadme.com.

Use when the user wants to share markdown content as a link others can read in the browser. Good for READMEs, PRDs, docs, proposals, notes, or any markdown content.

Pass the markdown content directly. If sharing a file, read it fi

title.description: Optional title for the document. If omitted, the first H1 heading is used.

markdown.description: The markdown content to share. Will be rendered as a readable web page.
